markers.title: Main-signal mast plate
markers.recognition: A mast plate attached below the signal head. The exact appearance varies, but it identifies the signal as having main-signal function.
markers.meaning: The signal can command a stop and must not be passed at Hp 0 without the appropriate authority or subsidiary indication.
playerAction: Treat a red aspect as a stop command.
Use the mast plate and nearby indicators to distinguish a main signal from a distant-only signal or repeater.
notes: Recognition-grade artwork is intentionally generic until route-specific plate variants are observed.
The database should record the actual plate design per route observation rather than assuming one universal appearance.

markers.title: Distant signal board
markers.recognition: A black-and-white chequered rectangular board, normally mounted with a signal that has distant-signal function.
markers.meaning: Identifies a distant signal or a signal carrying distant-signal information. It helps the player recognise that the displayed aspect announces conditions ahead rather than authorising passage as a main signal by itself.
playerAction: Read the displayed distant information and prepare for the announced condition.
Do not treat the board alone as a stop command.
Combine it with the white repeater or shortened-distance light when present.
notes: Exact proportions and mounting arrangements may vary.
A repeater should be identified from the complete assembly, including the lower white supplementary light, not from Ne 2 alone.
